title: Microsoft's Quantum Chip Just Got 1,000x More Stable
script: For 30 years, quantum computers have been plagued by a single fatal flaw — their qubits last milliseconds before collapsing from noise. Microsoft just cracked it. The new Majorana 2 chip, unveiled at Build 2026, uses lead superconductors instead of aluminum to shield qubits from interference. Average qubit lifetime: 20 seconds — 1,000 times more reliable than Majorana 1. Microsoft now says a full-scale quantum computer is on track by 2029.
